subject: DevOps
title: Managed Relational Databases
description: A 60-second lesson on Managed Relational Databases.

hook.text: Install Postgres on a VM and manage backups yourself, or click Create in RDS. Which sounds better?

buildup.text: Managed databases handle provisioning, patching, backups, and replication. You write the queries.

discovery.text: Automated backups and point-in-time recovery restore to any second in the last 35 days.

twist.text: Multi-AZ creates a standby replica. If the primary fails, failover happens in under a minute.

climax.text: Read replicas offload read traffic. Your primary handles writes while copies serve reads.

punchline.text: Managed DBs: reliable, backed up, replicated. Focus on data.

quiz.question: What happens during a Multi-AZ failover in a managed database?
options: Traffic automatically switches to a standby replica in another zone
The database shuts down until manually restarted
A new database is created from the latest backup
